We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer revert to General Fund the unexpended balance of capital outlay erroneously transferred to Trust Fund.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Provide appropriate adjusting entries in the GF books upon reversion of the unexpended balance of capital outlay erroneously transferred to TF and;
Record the unexpended LDRRMF QRF and MOOE balances in the Trust Liability-DRRM account in the TF books at year-end, even if there is no actual cash transfer yet at year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineering Office:
Issue the Certificate of Final Acceptance to the contractor only one year from the date of project completion, upon determination by the Inspectorate Team that no defects/failures were detected, or should there be any, these were satisfactorily complied with by the contractor concerned;
Require the contractors to post a warranty security against structural defects upon the issuance of the Certificate of Final Acceptance, rather than upon final payment.  To ensure that the warranty security is posted, we further recommend that the Municipality make it a policy to observe the one (1) year period after completion of the project before returning the retention money, unless substituted with a security, after which the warranty period shall commence.
Henceforth, observe the prescribed periods provided in Section 62.2.3.2 on the imposition of the warranty security, which is 2 to 15 years after final acceptance depending on the type of the infrastructure project.

observations.recommendation:
1.8. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) Re-classify the total costs of completed projects from the Construction in
Progress account to the appropriate PPE accounts based on the report of
the Municipal Engineer; and
b) compute and record the related depreciation on each completed PPEs.
1.9. We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer submit a report to the
Municipal Accountant immediately upon completion of each infrastructure
project, supported with the Certificate of Completion.
1.10. The Municipal Accountant committed to implement the recommendations while
the Municipal Engineer agreed to submit the required report to the Municipal
Accountant upon every completion of each infrastructure projects.

2.12. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, in coordination with the
Municipal Planning and Development Office and the Municipal Engineering
Office:
a.) Implement during the budget year all the identified projects in the Annual
Investment Plan that are to be funded from the 20% Development Fund;
b.) Include only in the AIP the projects/programs which are procurement-
and-implementation-ready to ensure their completion within the timetable
set in the AIP; and
c.) Take appropriate action against the defaulting contractor of the project on
Reblocking along Candaza Street.

2.13. During the exit conference, the Municipal Mayor informed the Audit Team, that
for the ensuing year, he already instructed all personnel concerned to observe a
specific schedule in the procurement of all projects during the year to ensure that
all the identified priority projects are either completed or must have been
commenced at the end of the year.

2.14. Moreover, he informed the Team that the Municipality has already sent a demand
for explanation to the defaulting contractor relating to the project on Reblocking
along Candaza Street and still waiting for its reply.

3.6. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, being the collecting arm of
the Municipality, with the support of the Municipal Mayor, conduct intensive
tax collection strategies and institute legal remedies on defaulting taxpayers
pursuant to R.A. 7160 to enhance collection efficiency.

3.7. The Municipal Treasurer acceded with the observation and volunteered that the
Treasury Services of the Municipality never failed in the roll out of information and
collection campaign. She also disclosed that she regularly submits to the Provincial
Treasury, for their appropriate action, the List of Delinquent Taxpayers.

4.7. We recommended that the Supply Officer gather all unserviceable properties
in the custody of the concerned personnel in one location and prepare the
Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties and such other
relevant documents.

4.8. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) constitute the members of the Disposal and Appraisal Committee with
instructions to initiate the disposal of unserviceable properties, and
recommend the mode of disposal;
b) file a request to COA, through the Audit Team Leader, to inspect and
review the agency appraisal on the said unserviceable properties; and
c) undertake the disposal of the said unserviceable properties after all the
requirements had been complied.

4.9. The Supply Officer agreed to the recommendation. The Municipal Mayor, during the
Exit Conference, informed the Audit Team that the Municipality complied with the
additional documentary requirements relating to the 2 heavy equipment which was
initially requested for disposal, and is due for submission to the Audit Team. Likewise,
they are now collating all the necessary documents for the eventual disposal of the
remaining unserviceable heavy equipment.

5.9. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, in coordination with the
MDRRM Officer, identify and include only in the LDRRM Plan the
appropriate Projects, Programs and Activities under each of the thematic
areas of disaster risk reduction and management in order that the LDRRMF
will be utilized in accordance with the purpose for which it was created and to
avoid audit disallowances.

5.10. The MDRRM Officer, in her reply to the Audit Observation Memorandum,
maintained that all of the identified PAPs embodied in the LDRRM Plan were
carefully identified, in coordination with stakeholders, in accordance with R.A.
10121. She likewise asserted that all rescue vehicles were used to respond to
accidents and emergencies, and in monitoring during weather disturbances and
such other DRRM activities.

5.11. It is worth emphasizing that, although the LGUs were given greater flexibility
towards disaster mitigation, preparation, response, rehabilitation and recovery by
virtue of R. A. 10121, Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated 25 March
2013 was issued to serve as a guide to LGUs in the allocation and use of the
LDRRMF and to enhance transparency and accountability in the use of the said
Fund.

5.12. Moreover, it is stressed that the use of the DRRM Vehicles was not restricted.
Rather, it was the ambiguity of the purposes and destinations contained in the trip
tickets that was raised. Hence, ensure that these are specific so as to avoid giving
an impression that the trips were for purposes other than those related with DRRM.

5.13. The Audit Team maintains its stand that the PAPs enumerated in Paragraph 5.6
hereof are outside the four (4) thematic areas of DRRM.

6.10. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Disbursement
Voucher, and the Municipal Treasurer issue the check, in favor of the Bureau of
Treasury, for the return of the unutilized fund from BFAR amounting to
₱279,727.50.

6.11. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the disallowed
amount with NFD to Prior Period Adjustment account.

6.12. During the Exit Conference, the Local Chief Executive disclosed that the corresponding
check relating to the unutilized fund from BFAR will have to be issued while the Municipal
Accountant agreed with the recommendation.

7.10. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the HRMO, revisit the
approved Organizational Structure and Staffing Pattern Plan and consider filling
up the vacant positions. Likewise, formulate policies relative to the hiring of Job
Order personnel.

7.11. The HRMO averred that policies on hiring of Job Order personnel has been initially
formulated in the ensuing year and will be reviewed for possible improvement. Also,
the Local Chief Executive disclosed that they will fill-up the vacant positions while
committing to improve its Organizational Structure and Staffing Pattern Plan in CY
2023.
